waterdeep.feature: The City of Splendors -- a vast metropolis built against the cliffs of the Sword Coast. Over a million souls crowd its streets, from the masked Lords of the palace to the pickpockets of Dock Ward.

neverwinter.feature: The Jewel of the North, still rebuilding after Mount Hotenow's eruption scarred the city decades ago. Warm ocean currents keep the harbor ice-free year-round.

baldurs_gate.feature: A sprawling port city where coin speaks louder than law. The Flaming Fist mercenary company keeps a tenuous peace between the Upper City's patriars and the Outer City's desperate poor.

daggerford.feature: A walled town at the mouth of the Delimbiyr River, governed by the young Duchess Morwen. Caravans bound for Waterdeep or Baldur's Gate stop here to resupply and hire guards.

amphail.feature: A sleepy horse-breeding village north of Waterdeep, named after a long-dead warlord whose statue still stands in the village green. Noble families from the city maintain country estates here.

routes.description: The Long Road north -- well-patrolled and lined with farms. Merchant caravans trundle past every hour.
The Trade Way south along the coast. Bandits and goblin raiding parties grow bolder the farther one gets from Waterdeep's patrols.
The Trade Way continues through the Fields of the Dead -- ancient battlefields where restless spirits have been known to waylay travelers after dark.
The High Road north -- a long and dangerous journey through Mere of Dead Men and the Sword Mountains. Only the well-armed or the desperate travel this route.
